title: Biology for Mortals
subtitle: Exploring Nature's Hidden Code
author: Wordsus Team
description: Explore the mysteries of life through 365 fascinating lessons. From DNA and genetics to evolution and deep ecology, explained simply for everyone.

chapters.title: What is life? Definition and key characteristics
chapters.description: Discover the fundamental biological concepts that separate living beings from inert matter and how science defines the beginning of life.

chapters.title: DNA revealed: The great molecule of heredity
chapters.description: We explore the structure of deoxyribonucleic acid, its famous double helix design, and how it stores our most essential genetic information.

chapters.title: The eukaryotic cell: Its structure and functions
chapters.description: We travel inside our own cells to learn about their organelles, their vital functions and how they cooperate to keep us alive every day.

chapters.title: Biological evolution: The mechanism of adaptation
chapters.description: We analyze the foundations of natural selection proposed by Darwin and how organisms slowly change over geological eras.

chapters.title: The Kingdom of Bacteria: Ubiquitous Microorganisms
chapters.description: Learn about the fascinating world of bacteria, the most abundant living beings on the planet, and understand their vital importance for all ecosystems.

chapters.title: Are viruses live? Your particular biological cycle
chapters.description: We discuss the true biological nature of viruses, their methods of invading host cells, and how they manage to multiply so effectively.

chapters.title: Fungi: The fascinating kingdom of fungi and yeasts
chapters.description: We discover the biology of fungi, from single-celled yeasts to the complex networks of mycelium that connect large natural forests.

chapters.title: The genetics of eye color in humans
chapters.description: We break down how our inherited genes interact to determine the amount of melanin in the iris and generate the different colors of our eyes.

chapters.title: Photosynthesis: How plants make their food
chapters.description: We study the biochemical process by which plants capture sunlight and carbon dioxide to produce their own energy and oxygen.

chapters.title: Animal migration: Biology of great journeys
chapters.description: We explore the incredible biological and physiological mechanisms that allow diverse species to navigate and survive journeys of thousands of kilometers.

chapters.title: Human blood groups and their hereditary basis
chapters.description: Understand the genetic difference between ABO system blood groups and the Rh factor, and why this compatibility is crucial in today's medicine.

chapters.title: The immune system: The body's defenses
chapters.description: We analyze the cells and processes that make up our immune system, which protects us from pathogens and diseases in a constant and silent way.

chapters.title: The biology of sleep: Why we sleep and its phases
chapters.description: We review neural processes, circadian rhythms, and the great biological importance of sleeping well to maintain optimal physical and mental health.

chapters.title: The process of digestion and absorption of nutrients
chapters.description: We accompany food on its journey through the human digestive system to understand how we extract and assimilate the energy necessary to live.

chapters.title: CRISPR-Cas9: The revolution in gene editing
chapters.description: Learn about the genetic editing tool inspired by the bacterial immune system that allows scientists to modify DNA sequences with precision.

chapters.title: Metamorphosis: Extreme biological transformations
chapters.description: Discover the impressive morphological and physiological changes that insects and amphibians go through during their deep development until the adult phase.

chapters.title: The biology of human aging at the cellular level
chapters.description: We analyze telomere shortening and oxidative stress to understand the cellular and genetic factors that cause our aging.

chapters.title: The biological bases of memory and learning
chapters.description: We explore how our neurons create new synaptic connections in the brain to process, store and retrieve the information we learn.

chapters.title: Symbiosis: Cooperation between different species
chapters.description: We study the fascinating symbiotic relationships, where different organisms evolve together to benefit each other and survive.

chapters.title: Extremophiles: Life in the most hostile environments
chapters.description: Meet the amazing microorganisms genetically and biologically adapted to thrive in conditions of extreme heat, high acidity or radiation.
